name: Mallard
description: The most common and recognizable duck in North America.
identification: Male has a glossy green head, yellow bill, and white neck ring. Female is mottled brown with an orange and black bill.
male_vs_female: Drakes have green heads and curly tail feathers; hens are drab brown for camouflage while nesting.
flight_pattern: Strong, steady wing beats; take off nearly vertically from water.
habitat: Found in almost any wetland habitat, including city parks and farm ponds.
behavior: Social ducks, often found in large flocks. They tip forward to feed on aquatic plants.
seasonal_patterns: Migrate in large flocks; many remain in the north as long as open water is available.
markers: Blue wing patch (speculum) with white borders on both sexes.

name: American Black Duck
description: A large, dark dabbling duck similar in size to a Mallard.
identification: Sooty brown body, pale grayish-brown head, and bright yellow-green bill (male).
male_vs_female: Sexes look similar, but males have brighter yellow bills while females have duller, olive-colored bills.
flight_pattern: Fast flight with white underwings contrasting sharply with dark body.
habitat: Salt marshes, coastal water, and freshwater wetlands in the East.
behavior: Wary and shy; often associates with Mallards but stays in more secluded areas.
seasonal_patterns: Winter primarily along the Atlantic coast.
markers: Dark body and pale head; purple-blue speculum lacks white borders.

name: Wood Duck
description: One of the most stunningly colorful waterfowl species.
identification: Male has a multi-colored crested head and red eyes. Female is gray-brown with a white eye-ring.
male_vs_female: Males are extremely colorful; females are subtle with a distinctive white teardrop around the eye.
flight_pattern: Agile flight through timber; head held high above the level of the body.
habitat: Wooded swamps, lakes, and rivers; nests in tree cavities.
behavior: Often perches in trees; shy and quick to take flight when disturbed.
seasonal_patterns: Early migrants in the fall; move to southern swamps for winter.
markers: Distinctive boxy crest on the head of both sexes.

name: Northern Pintail
description: An elegant, slender duck with long, pointed tail feathers.
identification: Male has a chocolate-brown head, white neck stripe, and long grey body.
male_vs_female: Males have the iconic 'pin' tail; females are slender with a long neck and tan head.
flight_pattern: Fast, graceful flyers; often form long, thin lines in flight.
habitat: Open wetlands, prairies, and tidal flats.
behavior: Very wary; feeds by dabbling and walking in stubble fields.
seasonal_patterns: One of the first ducks to migrate south in autumn.
markers: Long, pointed tail and slender profile.

name: Northern Shoveler
description: Known for its oversized, spatula-shaped bill.
identification: Large bill; male has green head, white chest, and rusty flanks.
male_vs_female: Male is colorful like a mallard but with a huge bill; female is mottled brown with the same large bill.
flight_pattern: Steady flight; wings make a rattling sound during take-off.
habitat: Shallow wetlands with high mud content for filtering food.
behavior: Sifts through mud and water with its specialized bill; often swims in circles to stir up food.
seasonal_patterns: Common in the West and South during winter.
markers: The unmistakable long, broad, spoon-shaped bill.

name: Gadwall
description: An understated but elegant gray-brown duck.
identification: Male is mostly gray with a black rear; female is similar to a female mallard but with a thinner bill.
male_vs_female: Males have a black 'butt' and fine gray patterns; females are brown with a white wing patch.
flight_pattern: Rapid wing beats; white speculum is a key field mark in flight.
habitat: Freshwater marshes and ponds with plenty of vegetation.
behavior: Often steals food from American Coots and other diving ducks.
seasonal_patterns: Widespread across North America; winters in the southern US and Mexico.
markers: White speculum (wing patch) visible in flight and sometimes at rest.

name: American Wigeon
description: A nervous, whistling duck often called 'Baldpate'.
identification: Male has a white forehead, green eye stripe, and pinkish-brown flanks.
male_vs_female: Male has the white 'bald' cap; female is gray-brown with a distinct dark smudge around the eye.
flight_pattern: Fast and erratic; shows large white shoulder patches in flight.
habitat: Wetlands, lakes, and often found grazing in fields like geese.
behavior: Highly vocal; males make a three-note whistle. Known for stealing food from divers.
seasonal_patterns: Migrates in small to medium-sized flocks.
markers: White patch on the forehead of the male; white wing coverts in flight.

name: Green-winged Teal
description: The smallest North American dabbling duck.
identification: Male has a chestnut head with a green ear patch and a vertical white stripe on the shoulder.
male_vs_female: Both have a bright green speculum; males are more colorful, females are mottled brown.
flight_pattern: Very fast and agile; flocks twist and turn in unison.
habitat: Shallow ponds and marshes with dense vegetation.
behavior: Feeds by mud-probing or dabbling in very shallow water.
seasonal_patterns: One of the last teals to migrate south; hardy for their size.
markers: Small size and bright green wing patch.

name: Blue-winged Teal
description: A small duck known for its early migration and blue wing patches.
identification: Male has a slate-blue head with a white crescent in front of the eye.
male_vs_female: Male has the facial crescent; both sexes show large powder-blue patches on the forewing.
flight_pattern: Swift and direct; shows blue wing patches prominently.
habitat: Ponds, marshes, and shallow lakes.
behavior: Highly migratory; often travels as far as South America for winter.
seasonal_patterns: First ducks to head south in the fall, often in August.
markers: Large blue patch on the front of the wing.

name: Cinnamon Teal
description: A western duck named for the male's rich cinnamon-red plumage.
identification: Male is entirely cinnamon-red with a dark back and red eyes.
male_vs_female: Male is striking red; female is nearly identical to a female Blue-winged Teal.
flight_pattern: Similar to Blue-winged Teal; fast with visible blue wing patches.
habitat: Small, shallow alkaline or freshwater marshes in the West.
behavior: Often seen in pairs or small groups; feeds on aquatic plants and insects.
seasonal_patterns: Mostly stays in the western US and Mexico.
markers: Male's deep cinnamon color and blue wing patches.

name: Mottled Duck
description: A non-migratory duck of the Gulf Coast and Florida.
identification: Similar to a female Mallard but darker, with a buffy throat and unstreaked face.
male_vs_female: Sexes look similar; males have a brighter yellow bill compared to the female's orange-black bill.
flight_pattern: Like a Mallard, but wing beats appear slightly faster.
habitat: Coastal salt marshes and nearby freshwater wetlands.
behavior: Usually found in pairs or small family groups; does not form large flocks.
seasonal_patterns: Non-migratory; remains in its coastal habitat year-round.
markers: Dark body, buffy throat, and blue-green speculum with no white borders.

name: Canvasback
description: The 'King of Ducks,' known for its distinctive sloping profile.
identification: Male has a chestnut head, black chest, and very white body. Long, sloping black bill.
male_vs_female: Male is bright white and red-headed; female is light brown with the same sloping profile.
flight_pattern: Powerful and fast; often fly in large V-formations.
habitat: Large lakes, bays, and estuaries; breeds in prairie potholes.
behavior: Dives for aquatic plants, especially wild celery.
seasonal_patterns: Winters in large rafts on coastal bays and the Great Lakes.
markers: Unique wedge-shaped head and sloping bill.

name: Redhead
description: A medium-sized diving duck with a round, cinnamon-red head.
identification: Male has a bright red head, black chest, and gray body. Blue bill with black tip.
male_vs_female: Male has red head; female is uniform brownish with a pale eye-ring.
flight_pattern: Rapid wing beats; flocks often fly in 'bunches' rather than lines.
habitat: Marshy lakes and bays; often winters in large numbers on the Gulf Coast.
behavior: Known for 'brood parasitism' - laying eggs in the nests of other ducks.
seasonal_patterns: Highly social in winter; thousands may gather in a single raft.
markers: Rounded red head and blue bill with a black tip.

name: Ring-necked Duck
description: Often found on smaller, shallower ponds than other diving ducks.
identification: Male is black above with gray sides and a white vertical stripe on the shoulder. Distinct white ring on bill.
male_vs_female: Male is black and gray; female is brown with a white eye-ring and bill ring.
flight_pattern: Fast flight; takes off more easily from water than most divers.
habitat: Wooded ponds, marshes, and rivers.
behavior: Dives for seeds and aquatic invertebrates; often found in small groups.
seasonal_patterns: Widespread migrant across North America.
markers: White ring near the tip of the bill (the 'ringed' neck is hard to see).

name: Greater Scaup
description: A large diving duck often found in huge rafts on coastal waters.
identification: Male has a rounded green-tinted black head, black chest, and white sides. Blue bill.
male_vs_female: Male has dark head and white sides; female has a white patch at the base of the bill.
flight_pattern: Strong flight; shows a long white stripe on the wing.
habitat: Large lakes and coastal bays; breeds in the Arctic.
behavior: Forms massive flocks (rafts) on open water during winter.
seasonal_patterns: Long-distance migrant from Arctic breeding grounds.
markers: Rounded head with green sheen; white wing stripe extends nearly to the tip.

name: Lesser Scaup
description: Very similar to the Greater Scaup but slightly smaller with a different head shape.
identification: Male has a peaked, purple-tinted black head. Blue bill with small black tip (nail).
male_vs_female: Male has peaked head; female has white patch at base of bill (like Greater Scaup).
flight_pattern: Swift; white wing stripe is shorter than on the Greater Scaup.
habitat: Lakes, ponds, and marshes; more common on freshwater than Greater Scaup.
behavior: Active diver; feeds on aquatic insects and mollusks.
seasonal_patterns: The most abundant diving duck in North America.
markers: Peaked head shape and small 'nail' on the tip of the bill.

name: Bufflehead
description: A tiny, energetic diving duck with a large, puffy head.
identification: Male is strikingly black and white with a large white patch on the back of the head.
male_vs_female: Male is bright white; female is dark gray with a small white cheek patch.
flight_pattern: Fast, low-flying, and energetic; wings beat very rapidly.
habitat: Small lakes and ponds; nests in old woodpecker holes.
behavior: Almost constantly diving; usually seen in small groups.
seasonal_patterns: Widespread in winter across most of the US.
markers: Small size and large white head patch on the male.

name: Common Goldeneye
description: Known as 'Whistler' because of the sound its wings make in flight.
identification: Male has a dark green-black head with a large round white spot near the bill. Bright yellow eye.
male_vs_female: Male has white face spot; female has a chocolate-brown head and gray body.
flight_pattern: Fast and direct; wings produce a distinct whistling sound.
habitat: Lakes, rivers, and coastal bays.
behavior: Aggressive divers; often found in small, tight-knit groups.
seasonal_patterns: Late migrant; often stays north as long as there is open water.
markers: Round white spot on the male's face and bright golden eye.

name: Barrow's Goldeneye
description: A western relative of the Common Goldeneye.
identification: Male has a crescent-shaped white spot on the face and more black on the back than the Common.
male_vs_female: Male has crescent face patch; female is very similar to Common Goldeneye hen.
flight_pattern: Strong, fast flight with whistling wings.
habitat: Mountain lakes and coastal waters of the West and Northeast.
behavior: Territorial during the breeding season.
seasonal_patterns: Mostly found in the Pacific Northwest and Rocky Mountains.
markers: Crescent-shaped white face patch on the male.

name: Ruddy Duck
description: A small, stiff-tailed duck with a comical appearance.
identification: Male in breeding plumage is rusty-red with a sky-blue bill and white cheeks.
male_vs_female: Male has blue bill and white cheeks; female is dusty brown with a dark line through the cheek.
flight_pattern: Buzzy flight low over the water; reluctant to fly.
habitat: Freshwater marshes and lakes with dense vegetation.
behavior: Often swims with its stiff tail cocked upright; very aggressive during courtship.
seasonal_patterns: Migrates throughout North America.
markers: Stiff, upright tail and (in males) bright blue bill.

name: Long-tailed Duck
description: A vocal sea duck known for its incredibly long tail feathers.
identification: Male is patterned in black, white, and gray with long tail streamers. Pattern changes significantly with seasons.
male_vs_female: Male has the long tail; female is mostly white and brown with no tail streamers.
flight_pattern: Fast flight low over the waves, often tilting from side to side.
habitat: Open ocean and large deep lakes (Great Lakes).
behavior: One of the deepest diving ducks, reaching depths of over 200 feet.
seasonal_patterns: Breeds in the Arctic tundra; winters along both coasts.
markers: Extremely long tail feathers on the male.

name: Surf Scoter
description: Often called the 'Skunk-head' because of the male's head pattern.
identification: Male is all black with white patches on the forehead and nape. Large, colorful bill.
male_vs_female: Male is black with white head patches; female is dark brown with two white patches on the cheek.
flight_pattern: Heavy flight in long lines or V's over the ocean.
habitat: Coastal waters and rocky shorelines.
behavior: Feeds primarily on mollusks, especially mussels, in the surf zone.
seasonal_patterns: Found along both the Atlantic and Pacific coasts in winter.
markers: Male's white head patches and large, multi-colored bill.

name: Black Scoter
description: The only North American duck with completely black plumage.
identification: Male is entirely black with a bright orange-yellow knob at the base of the bill.
male_vs_female: Male is all black; female is brown with a pale cheek contrasting with a dark cap.
flight_pattern: Usually flies in long, low strings over the water.
habitat: Coastal waters and large inland lakes during migration.
behavior: Highly vocal; the male's whistling call can be heard from a distance.
seasonal_patterns: Winters primarily along the coasts.
markers: Male's all-black body and bright orange bill knob.

name: White-winged Scoter
description: The largest of the three scoter species.
identification: Male is black with a small white 'comma' under the eye and white wing patches.
male_vs_female: Both sexes show white wing patches in flight; male has the white eye mark.
flight_pattern: Heavy, powerful flight; white wing patches are very prominent.
habitat: Coastal bays and large inland lakes.
behavior: Dives for mollusks and crustaceans; often stays further offshore than other scoters.
seasonal_patterns: Migrates across the continent from northern breeding grounds.
markers: White wing patch (speculum) and male's white eye comma.

name: Common Eider
description: A massive sea duck, the largest in the Northern Hemisphere.
identification: Male has a white back, black belly, and a pale green patch on the nape. Sloping profile.
male_vs_female: Male is white and black; female is heavily barred with brown and black.
flight_pattern: Strong, steady flight low over the water; very bulky appearance.
habitat: Rocky northern coasts and islands.
behavior: Highly social; feeds on mussels and other shellfish by diving.
seasonal_patterns: Moves south to the New England and mid-Atlantic coasts in winter.
markers: Large size and unique sloping profile from bill to forehead.
